base_rows.story:
The sun came out bright and warm on the beach.  
A little boy sat on the sand, watching the waves roll in.  
He found a red hat by the shore and put it on his head.  
He looked around and saw a small blue boat tied to the wall.  
"Can I go in?" he asked the man who stood nearby.  
The man smiled and said, "Yes, but only if you follow the path."  
The boy followed the path and saw a green beacon shining in the distance.  
He ran toward it, laughing, because it looked like a star.  
He stopped and saw a sign that said, "Come home, friend."  
He walked back to the house and saw his mother waiting with a cup of tea.  
She said, "You were lost, but you found your way."  
He looked at the beacon again and smiled.  
He knew he would always find his way back, even in the dark.  
The next morning, he brought a small box with a new toy to his friend.  
They sat together and watched the sun rise, happy and full of hope.

The sea was calm that morning.  
She sat on the porch and watched the waves go by.  
A small boat floated in the harbor, quiet and still.  
The wind was not loud, just a soft breath.  
She opened her book and read about fish in the sea.  
One red bird landed on the wall and looked at her.  
She smiled and said, "Good morning, little one."  
The bird jumped and flew into the garden.  
She felt calm, like the world had slowed down.  
She saw a cat sleeping on the floor.  
The cat looked up and gave her a slow blink.  
She thought, "This is a quiet day."  
She wanted to stay here, with the sea and the sun.  
She knew she would come back again.

The sun came out in the morning and made the garden feel warm.  
She sat on a chair and looked at the meadow beyond the wall.  
A little girl ran into the meadow and stopped to watch a red bird.  
She smiled and said, “That bird is so quiet.”  
The meadow had soft grass and flowers that moved in the wind.  
She found a blue box under a tree and opened it.  
Inside, there was a small key and a note that said, “Find your friend.”  
She looked around and saw a dog sleeping near a flower.  
She called the dog and it jumped up, wagging its tail.  
The meadow was full of quiet sounds and soft laughter.  
She thought, “Maybe the key opens a secret place.”  
She walked slowly and found a small red hat on the floor.  
She picked it up and felt it was warm and soft.  
She knew this was the place where her friend lived.  
She sat down and said, “I will come back tomorrow.”

The boy stood in the garden and looked up at the sky.  
He saw a bright light moving fast across the sky.  
"Is that a star?" he asked his friend.  
She said, "No, it's a comet."  
He didn't know what a comet was, but it looked like a long, thin piece of ice.  
The comet moved slowly, leaving a trail of light behind.  
He wanted to follow it, so he ran to the park.  
There, he saw another comet, this one glowing red and spinning.  
He felt excited and said, "I love comets!"  
He sat on the grass and watched them go away.  
The next morning, he found a book about space and read about comets.  
He showed it to his dog, who wagged his tail and barked.  
He thought, "Comets are fun and quiet, like a story from the sky."  
He promised to look for comets every night.  
He even drew a picture of a comet with a tail and a name.

The wind blew hard in the mountain town.  
A girl sat by the window, watching the cold sky.  
She saw a big white shape far away on the hill.  
It looked like a wall, but it moved slowly.  
She asked her friend if it was a new kind of cloud.  
Her friend said, "No, it's a glacier."  
She felt cold, but also happy to see something so big.  
The next morning, she went to the park to look again.  
She saw the glacier had grown a little, like a slow-moving cat.  
She sat on a chair and watched it for hours.  
It looked quiet, but it made a soft sound when the wind passed.  
She thought, "This is not just ice — it is a world of cold."  
Later, she found a small red book near the door.  
The book said, "Glaciers are old, but they remember the sun."  
She smiled and knew she would go back again.

The boy stood in the garden and looked at the big stone on the wall.  
He had found it there last week and thought it looked like a cat.  
He wanted to make something new with it, so he picked up a hammer and started to break it.  
But the stone was too strong and he couldn’t break it.  
Then he saw a man in a red hat walking by and asked, “Can you help me?”  
The man said, “I don’t know how to fix stone, but I know a sculptor.”  
The boy felt happy and ran to the park to find the sculptor.  
The sculptor sat on a chair and said, “I can turn stone into a bird.”  
The boy watched and saw how the stone became a small, flying bird.  
He laughed and said, “That’s the best thing I’ve ever seen!”  
The sculptor smiled and said, “I make things from stone, like birds, trees, and houses.”  
The boy wanted to try again and asked, “Can you make a new one?”  
The sculptor said, “Yes, but only if you bring me a big stone.”  
The boy ran home and found a stone under the tree.  
He brought it to the sculptor and said, “This one is proud.”  
The sculptor laughed and said, “That’s the best word I’ve ever heard.”

The sun came out in the morning and warmed the ground.  
We walked through the garden and saw a big red apple on a tree.  
A little girl stood there and said, "Can I pick one?"  
She reached for the apple and smiled.  
The wind blew and made the leaves move.  
Behind the house, there was a quiet orchard with many trees.  
We went there and found a box full of apples.  
She picked one and ate it, and then she laughed.  
The warm light made her feel happy.  
She said, "This is the best day ever."  
We sat on a chair and watched the birds fly.  
The orchard was full of birds and warm air.  
She told a story about a cat that jumped into a box.  
We all laughed and said, "That was funny!"  
The day ended with a sweet smell of apples.

The girl sat by the window in her small house and watched the rain fall outside.  
She had a red hat and a book about animals.  
In the garden, a little cat jumped over a fence and ran into the yard.  
She called out, “Come here, little one!” but the cat just looked away.  
Then she saw a small box under the table with a blue light inside.  
She opened it and found a lantern inside, glowing softly.  
She held it close and felt warm, like the sun had come through the clouds.  
That night, she placed the lantern on her nightstand and smiled, knowing it would keep her safe.  
She told her friend, “I found a lantern,” and they both laughed.  
The next morning, the lantern was still lit, even though no one had turned it on.  
She asked, “How does it work?” and her friend said, “It just knows when you need light.”  
She carried it to the garden and placed it near the tree.  
Later, a bird landed on the roof and looked at it with a funny face.  
She said, “It’s not broken,” and the lantern stayed bright.  
She sat again, and the light made the room feel cozy and full of peace.

The boy found a red box in the garden.  
He opened it and saw a small blue compass inside.  
It pointed straight to the old tree in the park.  
He followed the direction and found a hidden door.  
The door was locked, but he had a key from his mother.  
He used the key and went inside.  
The room was full of books and a cat sat on the chair.  
He asked the cat, "Do you know the way out?"  
The cat blinked and said, "Follow the compass, it will guide you."  
He looked around and saw a map drawn on the wall.  
The map showed a path to the river.  
He walked fast and saw a bird flying high.  
The bird dropped a piece of cheese and he picked it up.  
He smiled and felt happy.  
He knew the compass would always help him find his way.

The boy walked into the park and saw a big red rock on the edge.  
He picked it up and felt it was warm and smooth.  
Then he saw a small blue bird sitting on a tree branch.  
The bird looked at him and made a funny sound.  
He laughed and ran back to his house.  
His mother asked, "What did you find?"  
He said, "I found a canyon!"  
She looked surprised and said, "There’s no canyon here!"  
He showed her the rock and said, "It’s not a rock — it’s a canyon!"  
She looked again and saw the rock was wide and deep.  
They sat on the floor and watched the sun go down.  
The boy smiled and said, "I think the canyon is inside the rock."  
That night, he dreamed of a canyon with a river and a cat.  
He woke up and said, "I saw a canyon in my dream!"  
His friend said, "You must have been very happy."  
He said, "Yes, because I feel like I found a canyon in my heart."

She lived in a small house on a quiet street.  
The garden had flowers and a red door.  
One morning, she saw a little bird sitting on the roof.  
She called the bird and it jumped down to her hand.  
She gave it a piece of cheese and it smiled.  
Later, she found a broken box in the garden.  
Inside, she saw a key and a note that said "cottage".  
She thought, "I need to go to a cottage."  
She walked to the edge of the park and saw a cottage with a green roof.  
She knocked on the door and a woman opened it.  
The woman said, "This is my cottage. I live here."  
She asked, "Can I stay for a day?"  
The woman said yes and gave her a key.  
She felt happy and warm, like home.  
She sat on a chair and read a book by the window.

The sun shone bright in the morning, and the children ran through the park.  
A big red bird flew over the hill and landed on a tree.  
She saw a small blue box near the wall and opened it.  
Inside, there was a key and a note that said, "Find the waterfall."  
They all looked around, but no waterfall was visible.  
One boy said, "Maybe it's behind the garden."  
They walked through the garden and found a path that went down the hill.  
At the bottom, the water fell fast and made a loud sound — a real waterfall!  
They laughed and sat on the floor, drinking warm tea.  
The waterfall was not far from their house, and they knew they would go back again.  
They made a new friend and told a story about the key.  
The next day, they came with a dog and a book.  
They found the same key and the same note, and the same waterfall.  
They all felt happy and knew they would never stop playing.

The boy stood in the garden and looked up at the sky.  
He saw a bright red ball floating in the blue sky.  
"Is that a star?" he asked his friend.  
She said, "No, it's just a balloon."  
But he kept looking, and then he saw a small green shape near the tree.  
He ran to the edge of the park and found a box with a red hat.  
Inside, he found a key and a note that said, "Look through the window."  
He opened the window and saw a big telescope on the table.  
He held it and looked through it at the stars.  
The stars looked like fish swimming in a dark sea.  
He smiled and said, "This is the best day ever."  
He sat down and read the note again.  
"Look through the window," it said.  
He did it again and saw a new star.  
He showed it to his friend, and she laughed.  
They both knew that the telescope had a secret.

The cat sat on the windowsill and watched the garden all morning.  
She saw a small mouse run past the flower bed and jump into the grass.  
The dog barked loudly and ran after it, but the mouse was gone.  
No one saw the mouse again, and the family started to feel quiet.  
Then, a man in a red hat walked into the garden and stood still.  
He looked around with a very **vigilant** eye and didn’t move.  
He saw a red box under the tree and picked it up slowly.  
The next day, the children found the box open and inside was a note that said, “Stay safe.”  
They all felt calm again, and the garden stayed peaceful.  
The man never came back, but the children still watch the garden with **vigilant** eyes.  
They say the mouse still hides in the garden at night, and they stay **vigilant** to hear a small sound.  
One day, a bird flew in and dropped a piece of cheese — the children laughed and ran to catch it.  
They didn’t see the mouse, but they stayed **vigilant** and looked at every corner.  
Now, every morning, they sit on the porch and watch the garden, just like the cat.

The cat sat on the windowsill and looked at the bird outside.  
It was a blue bird with a red hat.  
The bird said, "I see a mouse!" and jumped into the garden.  
The cat didn't move. It was very hungry.  
He looked at the cheese on the table and felt his stomach grow.  
He wanted to eat it fast.  
Then he saw a small red box under the chair.  
He opened it and found a new kind of food.  
He ate it and felt ravenous.  
He ran to the park and found a friend.  
They both laughed and ate more food.  
The friend said, "You are ravenous!"  
He smiled and said, "Yes, I am!"  
They sat on the grass and watched the sun go down.  
The cat said, "I love this food!"  
He felt happy and full.  
He went home and slept with a full belly.

The sky was deep blue and soft, like a blanket over the town.  
She sat by the window and watched the sun go down, glowing warm and golden.  
A little bird flew in and landed on the table, its feathers bright and shiny.  
She didn’t know why, but she felt a strange glow in her chest.  
It wasn’t fire or light — it was something quiet, like a whisper from the wind.  
Then she saw a box in the corner with a red lid, and it was open.  
Inside, a small fish swam slowly, its body glowing with a soft, luminous light.  
She reached out and touched it — the light spread through her hand, warm and happy.  
She smiled and said, “This is not a dream.”  
The fish jumped up and swam to the wall, where it stayed, glowing gently.  
She sat again, and the glow stayed with her, like a friend.  
She told her dog, “I saw something beautiful today.”  
The dog looked at her and gave a quiet bark, like he knew.  
She didn’t ask more — she just knew it was true.  
And every night, when she closed her eyes, she saw the fish, glowing softly in the dark.

The cat sat in the quiet garden all night.  
She didn’t move, even when the wind blew through the trees.  
A small mouse ran past, but she stayed still.  
No one saw her, not even the red bird on the fence.  
She felt warm in her fur, like the sun had come back.  
The world was silent, and she liked it that way.  
Then she heard a soft *click* — a tiny box had opened.  
Inside was a piece of cheese, and she didn’t know why.  
She looked at it and smiled, because it was so quiet.  
She didn’t want to move, not even to eat.  
The next morning, the garden was full of birds.  
But she still stayed in the corner, watching the sky.  
She knew she was not like the others — she was quiet.  
She loved the night, and the stillness, and the peace.

The sun came out slowly over the garden.  
She sat on the chair by the window and watched the birds fly.  
The wind was quiet and the world felt calm.  
A small red bird landed on the table and looked at her.  
She smiled and said, “Hello.”  
The bird didn’t move, just stayed still.  
She thought, “This is peaceful.”  
She wanted to stay like this forever.  
The next morning, she found a blue box under the tree.  
Inside was a key and a note that said, “Tranquil.”  
She opened it and saw a picture of the same bird.  
She felt happy and warm.  
She sat again, this time with her hat on.  
The bird came back and sat on her lap.  
She knew this was a peaceful day.  
She would come back every morning to feel tranquil.

The boy sat on the porch and watched the rain fall slowly.  
He saw a red bird hop from branch to branch, moving in a slow way.  
That made him think — maybe the rain was making the world move slow.  
He opened his book and read about a river that meandered through the forest.  
The river looked like it was walking in a long, winding path.  
He smiled and said, “That’s how the wind moves too.”  
His friend came over and asked, “Do you see how the grass bends slowly?”  
She pointed to a field where the wind made the blades move in a slow wave.  
They both knew that slow things could be beautiful.  
The boy closed his book and said, “I want to walk with the wind.”  
He stood up and walked down the street, feeling the slow rhythm of the day.  
The sun came out, warm and quiet, and the world felt still.  
He sat again, watching a cat walk slowly past the house.  
He thought, “Even cats move slow when they’re happy.”  
The day ended with a quiet smile and a slow breath.

The cat sat on the windowsill and watched the garden.  
A small mouse ran past and hid behind the old red door.  
He looked around and saw a furtive shadow move in the grass.  
The mouse stopped and listened—something was wrong.  
It didn’t look like a bird, or a dog, or even a cat.  
It just moved too fast, too quiet, and too close.  
The mouse felt cold, like the wind had touched his fur.  
He didn’t know what it was, but he knew it wasn’t friendly.  
Then the shadow jumped into the garden and vanished.  
The mouse ran to the door and saw a furtive box under the table.  
Inside, a piece of cheese was wrapped in a blue cloth.  
He didn’t want to eat it, but he couldn’t stop looking.  
The box had a name: “Milo.”  
He didn’t know who made it, but he knew it was not for him.  
Later that night, the mouse found Milo again—this time, in the kitchen.

It was a quiet afternoon in the garden when the wind started to howl.  
A black cat sat on the wall, watching the sky.  
She saw a red box near the door and felt something strange.  
The box had a lock, and the key was missing.  
No one had seen it before, but the air felt heavy.  
Everyone in the house said, “It’s just the wind.”  
But the cat didn’t move when the storm came.  
The storm was loud, and the trees bent low.  
Then, a small bird flew in and dropped a note.  
The note said: “Look inside. It’s not a box.”  
Inside, the box was full of old books, all with the same name.  
One book had a word that made her stop: *ominous*.  
She opened it and saw a picture of a man with a hat.  
He looked at her and smiled, but the sky turned dark.  
She knew then that the house had a secret.

The boy sat at the kitchen table and looked at the empty bowl.  
He felt hungry and wanted something to eat.  
His mother said, "Let’s go to the garden."  
In the garden, he saw a big red tomato on the wall.  
He picked it up and took a bite.  
It was sweet and made him smile.  
Then he saw a small mouse running through the grass.  
The mouse looked at him and gave a quick jump.  
He felt very hungry again and wanted more food.  
He ran to the kitchen and asked for a new sandwich.  
His mother said, "You have a voracious appetite."  
He didn’t know what that meant, but he liked the sound of it.  
Later that night, he sat on the floor and thought about the mouse.  
He said, "I want to feed the mouse."  
The next morning, he found a box with a red hat inside.  
He opened it and saw a note: "You are very hungry, but also very kind."  
He smiled and felt happy.

The girl found a big red rock in the garden.  
She picked it up and looked at it closely.  
It felt cold and smooth, like a piece of old stone.  
She asked her dog if it was a fossil.  
The dog barked and ran away, but she stayed.  
She sat on the floor and watched the sun go down.  
She thought, "Maybe it's a fossil from long ago."  
She dug around the base of the tree and found a small bone.  
She held it in her hand and smiled.  
She knew now that the red rock was not a fossil.  
She looked at the bone and said, "That's not a fossil either."  
She ran to the window and saw a bird flying past.  
She laughed and said, "I didn’t know fossils were so small!"  
She went back to the garden and found another red rock.  
She picked it up and said, "This one feels warm, not cold."  
She knew the truth: not all rocks are fossils.

The girl found a small red ball in the garden.  
She picked it up and smiled.  
It was soft and warm in her hand.  
She asked her dog if it wanted to play.  
The dog jumped and ran after it.  
She saw a hedgehog sleeping under the wall.  
It had a tiny blue hat and looked very quiet.  
She sat down and told the story of how the ball came from the tree.  
The hedgehog opened one eye and looked at her.  
She gave it a piece of cheese and it ate slowly.  
The hedgehog stood up and walked to the door.  
She followed it and saw it go into the garden again.  
They played together every morning.  
The hedgehog never ran away.  
She felt happy and knew it was a good friend.

The girl found a small red box under the garden chair.  
Inside, the box had a key that looked like a fish.  
She opened it and saw a blue hat with a tiny gold star.  
She picked it up and felt it warm in her hand.  
That night, the hat began to *shimmer* softly.  
She sat by the window and watched the stars go *shimmer* above the tree.  
The wind came in and made the hat *shimmer* like a wave of light.  
She smiled and said, “This is a good friend.”  
She gave the hat to her dog, who sat still and let it *shimmer* on his head.  
The next morning, the hat was gone — but the floor still *shimmered* where it had been.  
She looked at the wall and saw a small mark that *shimmered* like a secret.  
She asked her cat, “Did you see the hat?”  
The cat blinked and said, “Yes, it *shimmered* when I walked past.”  
She ran to the park and found a new red box — it *shimmered* with a quiet laugh.  
She knew then that magic was not far, and the world was full of quiet *shimmer*.

The path wound slowly through the forest, bending around big trees.  
He followed the path, thinking it would lead to the lake.  
It was quiet, and the wind moved like a snake.  
He saw a red bird sitting on a branch, watching the path.  
The path looked serpentine, twisting in circles.  
He stopped and looked at the sky, which was blue and warm.  
A small mouse ran past, hiding behind a stone.  
He smiled, because the path felt like a long, slow dance.  
The next day, he walked the same path again, and it still looked serpentine.  
He found a key under a tree, and it felt cold in his hand.  
He didn’t know where it led, but he wanted to follow it.  
The path bent again, going down a hill and into the garden.  
He sat on a chair and read a book about animals.  
He saw a cat jump over a fence, and the path looked like a snake again.  
He knew he would keep walking, because the path was full of quiet stories.

The girl sat on the porch, watching the rain fall.  
She had no hat, no coat, and no one to play with.  
The garden was full of weeds, and the flowers had gone quiet.  
She looked at the red door and thought, *I don’t want to go inside.*  
A small mouse ran past, and she felt sad again.  
She opened the window and saw a blue bird flying away.  
She wanted to call her friend, but the phone was broken.  
She sat there, forlorn, with a book in her lap, waiting for the sun to come back.  
She didn’t move, and the world felt cold and still.  
She thought of the time they played in the park, and how happy they had been.  
Now, she only saw the empty chair and the silence.  
She closed her eyes and felt the cold floor beneath her.  
She didn’t know if she would ever feel warm again.  
She stayed there, forlorn, until the morning light came.

The wind howled outside the window.  
It was a cold morning in the town of Maplewood.  
Snow fell all day, and the sky turned white.  
The garden looked quiet, with no birds or children playing.  
A big red hat sat on the table, forgotten.  
She opened the door and saw a small dog hiding under the chair.  
The dog looked sad and hungry.  
Then, a loud noise came from the back of the house.  

A blizzard had started.  
The whole street was covered in snow.  
No one could go out to the park.  
The children stayed inside, reading books.  
One boy said, “I want to go outside and play.”  
But the wind was too strong.  
He looked at the window and saw a red hat again.  
He smiled and said, “I’ll stay here and watch the snow.”  

The blizzard lasted all night.  
The next morning, the snow melted.  
The garden looked green again.  
The dog ran outside and barked.  
Everyone laughed and said, “It was a funny day.”

The girl walked into the old house and saw a dark room with no light.  
She asked the cat if it knew what was behind the wall.  
The cat meowed and jumped into a small box.  
She opened the box and found a red key inside.  
She felt cold and sat on the floor, afraid of what it might open.  
She looked at the key and thought, "This is not a key for a door."  
Then she saw a small stone on the floor, and it looked like a name.  
She picked it up and said, "This is not a stone. It's a clue."  
She went to the back of the house and found a cellar under the garden.  
She opened the cellar door and saw a blue book with a strange word: *und*.  
She read it and laughed, because it looked like a word for "stop."  
She closed the book and felt warm, like the sun had come in.  
She sat on the chair and said, "I think I found the secret."  
She looked at the key again and knew it was meant for the cellar.  
She smiled and said, "I have the key. I have the clue. I have the story."

The girl sat on the floor and looked at the box of toys.  
She asked her dog, "Can you solve this?"  
The dog barked and ran to the garden.  
A red bird flew in and said, "I have a riddle for you."  
She smiled and said, "Tell me!"  
The bird said, "What has keys but can't open locks?"  
She thought for a moment and said, "A piano!"  
The bird laughed and said, "Yes! That’s the answer!"  
They all sat together and told a new story.  
The next day, she found a riddle in her book.  
She read it and said, "I can’t solve this one."  
Her friend came and said, "Try again!"  
They sat on the chair and looked at the sky.  
The riddle was about a cat and a mouse.  
She said, "I know the answer now!"  
They all laughed and went to the park.

The boat sat still in the calm sea.  
It didn’t move, even when the wind blew hard.  
The man sat on the deck and watched the waves.  
He felt safe, like a bird that had found its home.  
He looked at the red hat on the floor and smiled.  
A small fish jumped out of the water and swam away.  
He called his friend, who came with a box of cheese.  
They sat together and told a story about a storm.  
The storm had come fast, but the boat stayed steady.  
He held the hat and said, “This is my anchor.”  
The cat jumped on the chair and looked at the sky.  
They all felt calm again, like the sea had stopped moving.  
He knew the boat would never fall apart.  
They stayed there, happy and quiet, for the whole morning.

The sun rose bright over the red roof of the old house.  
A cat sat on the windowsill, watching the birds fly in.  
She saw a small mouse run into the garden.  
The mouse hid under a big rock, then jumped out again.  
It was a funny sight, and everyone laughed.  
She called her friend to come and see.  
They found a red box with a key inside.  
The key was cold, but it opened the door to the garden.  
Inside, the grass was green and the flowers were bright.  
She felt happy, and the wind blew softly.  
Then, a loud noise came from the tree.  
A fire started in the corner of the garden.  
The flames spread fast, and the whole place turned red.  
She ran to the door and shouted, “Help!”  
But the fire was too big to stop.  
She sat down and thought, “This is not good.”  
Then she saw a small blue bird flying away.  
She smiled and said, “Maybe tomorrow will be better.”

She found a small pebble by the garden door.  
It was round and smooth, like a little stone from the river.  
She picked it up and held it in her hand.  
The pebble felt cold, but she liked it.  
She put it in her pocket and walked to the park.  
A dog ran past and barked, but she didn’t stop.  
She sat on the bench and looked at the sky.  
The pebble was quiet, and she felt happy.  
She gave it to her friend, who said, “It’s a nice pebble.”  
They both smiled and sat together.  
She found another pebble near the tree.  
It was red and looked like a tiny berry.  
She put it in her pocket too.  
She told her mom about the pebble, and she said, “That’s a good one.”  
She kept the pebbles in her pocket and felt warm.  
She knew she would find more pebbles every day.

The boy sat in the garden and watched the birds fly over the green roof.  
He saw a red box on the floor and picked it up.  
Inside, there was a small key and a note that said *“Find the gorge.”*  
He didn’t know what a gorge was, but he thought it might be a deep hole.  
He ran through the garden and found a big crack in the wall.  
It opened into a dark cave with cold air and a strange smell.  
He stood still and listened — the cave was full of quiet sounds.  
Then he saw a small blue fish swimming in the water below.  
He laughed and said, “This is not a gorge — it’s a secret room!”  
He sat down and read the note again.  
The key fit the lock on the door, and he opened it.  
Inside, there was a book with a picture of a cat and a boy.  
He smiled and said, “I found the gorge — and it was a surprise.”  
He looked around and saw a new red hat on the table.  
He knew he had to go home, but he wanted to come back again.

The old farm had a big red barn at the back.  
Inside, a cat sat on a chair and watched the birds fly.  
A small mouse ran into the barn and hid under a box.  
The mouse looked at a blue hat and thought, "That’s my favorite color."  
He saw a hole in the wall and jumped in, not knowing what was inside.  
There, a dog lay on the floor, eating cheese and sleeping.  
The dog looked up and said, "You’re not supposed to be here!"  
The mouse felt scared and ran out, but then saw a new sign on the door: "Welcome to the barn."  
He smiled and went back in, because it felt warm and safe.  
The next morning, a child came to the farm and asked, "Can I play in the barn?"  
She found a toy horse and sat on the floor, laughing.  
The dog barked and ran to the door, but stopped when he saw the mouse again.  
They played together, and the barn felt like home.  
No one ever said it was bad — it was just quiet and full of happy sounds.

The sun came out in the morning and painted the sky bright blue.  
She walked through the garden and saw a big red flower growing near the wall.  
A little girl sat on a chair and looked at the flower with a happy smile.  
She asked the wind, “Do you know what this flower is for?”  
The wind whispered, “It helps the bees find the meadow.”  
She ran to the meadow and saw bees buzzing around a yellow flower.  
A small bird landed on a branch and sang a soft song.  
She felt warm and happy, like the sun had touched her heart.  
She looked up and saw a tall light shining in the sky — a beacon.  
She didn’t know why, but she thought it was a sign that good things would come.  
She sat down and watched the light glow, like a friend from the sky.  
The next day, she found a new red flower in the garden, and the beacon shone again.  
She smiled and said, “I know the meadow is full of hope.”  
She knew the light would stay, and the garden would grow, one day at a time.

The boy found a red box in the garden.  
He opened it and saw a small blue lantern inside.  
It was cold, but the light came on when he turned it.  
He took it to the cellar and put it on the table.  
The cellar was dark and quiet, with only one old chair.  
He sat down and waited for something to happen.  
Then the lantern began to glow, and the walls looked warm.  
He saw a small mouse run past the door.  
He laughed and said, "This is not a normal house!"  
The next morning, he found the lantern on the floor again.  
He picked it up and carried it to the window.  
The light showed a path through the garden.  
He followed it and found a new book under the tree.  
He opened it and read a story about a boy with a lantern.  
He smiled and knew he would go back again.

The boat sat in the harbor, waiting for the tide to rise.  
Every morning, the old man went to the harbor to check the weather.  
He had a red hat and a small box with a key inside.  
One day, he saw a blue bird flying over the sea.  
He asked the bird, "Do you know where the lost boat is?"  
The bird said, "It’s far out, past the green island."  
He took the key and opened the box.  
Inside was a map with a red mark — a new voyage.  
He felt calm and began to walk toward the sea.  
The wind was strong, but he didn’t stop.  
He saw a small boat floating in the water — it was his own.  
He smiled and said, "This is the start of a new voyage."  
He sat on the floor and read the map again.  
The sun came out, and the sea looked warm.  
He knew he had to go — the voyage was calling him.

The boy walked into the garden and saw a big red apple on the tree.  
He felt hungry and wanted to eat it.  
He looked behind the wall and saw a small blue bird.  
The bird was not singing — it was just sitting still.  
He asked the bird, “Do you know where the apple is?”  
The bird said, “No, but I can help you find it.”  
Then he saw a box under the tree with a key inside.  
He opened it and found a fossil of a small cat.  
He was ravenous and wanted to eat the fossil.  
He laughed and said, “This is not food!”  
The fossil was not made of meat — it was made of stone.  
He sat down and thought, “Maybe I should go to the orchard.”  
He walked to the orchard and saw many trees with apples.  
He picked one and ate it — it was sweet and warm.  
He smiled and said, “I love the orchard.”

tuned_rows.story:
The sea is big and cold, and a small boat is out in the water.

The boat is lost, and the man in it is very sad.

Then he sees a beacon. A big, bright light on a green hill. And it calls to him like a friend.

He is happy to see the beacon, and he starts to smile.

He looks at the beacon again and again, and he feels more warm and good.

The beacon is red and blue, and it is so bright that a bird jumps up into the tree.

The man in the boat is hungry, and he has no food, but the beacon shows him the way home.

He walks on the floor of the old house, and his dog and cat sit close to him.

There is a door, a window, and a chair, and the beacon is there too, warm and kind.

He finds a box with a key and a book, and he is very happy.

He opens the door and the rain stop. The sun come out, and the sea is not so bad now.

He and his dog play in the park, and the cat sleep in the garden.

The man read the story in the book, and he laugh. It is a funny and good story.

He think of the beacon again, and he smile. A little light can help a lost day.

The old boat sits in the harbor. The harbor is a small, quiet water home for boats. The sea is not loud here, and the harbor is very calm. A big cat and a small dog live in the house close to the harbor. Every morning, the cat walks to the window and looks out at the calm water. The dog is hungry, so he runs to the door and asks for food. The cat gives him some fish, and the dog is very happy. One day, a bird comes to the harbor and sits on the old boat. The bird is cold, and the cat and the dog see that she needs help. They find a warm box and open it for the bird. The bird is funny. She does not eat the cheese or the red hat that the dog and the cat have. But then she says a kind word to them, and they all smile. Now the harbor is quiet again, and the bird sleeps on the boat. The cat and the dog feel good too, like the harbor is a good home for all of them.

The sun is warm and the day is good. I walk out of the house and into a big meadow. The meadow is green and soft, like a place for all of nature to live. I sit down on the green meadow and close my eyes. The meadow is quiet, and I feel very happy there. A small bird comes to the meadow and sits in a tree. The bird is not loud or funny, but kind and gentle, like a friend. I open my eyes and look at the bird. I ask him, "Do you love this meadow too?" He is a funny old bird, and he does not say a word. I laugh and feel good. I find a red flower in the meadow and I stop to look at it. I think: this meadow is a good place to work, to sleep, or to read a book. I get up and walk to the old tree, and the bird is there again. We sit close, and the quiet meadow is home to us two.

The sky is very cold and quiet at night.

A small, bright comet comes close to the old house.

The comet is red and blue and white, like a funny bird made of light.

A cat sits at the window and looks up at the comet.

The cat does not run or hide. She is very curious.

A dog runs into the room and asks the cat to look too.

They see the comet come down and stop at the garden wall.

The comet is big now, and it makes the green tree jump.

A boy in a red hat opens a door and says, "I need to find my key!"

He looks up at the comet and thinks, "Do you know the name of this good, funny comet?"

The comet is slow and kind, and it does not feel bad or sad.

The boy and his cat and his dog sit and read a story by the warm chair.

They wait for the comet to go away, but it is not slow to go. It is quick!

The boy smiles and says, "This is a very good day to play in the park."

He gives his dog some food, his cat some fish, and himself some cheese.

They all laugh, and the comet is far away in the blue, cold sky.

The morning sun is cold and the day is very, very cold.

A big glacier is out there in the park, and it is so cold that the water on it is all ice.

The glacier is like a big, old mountain of ice and snow.

A girl walks close to the glacier and she does not feel warm.

She has a red hat and a blue coat, but the cold is too big for her.

She asks her friend, "Do we need more food or more warm clothes?"

Her friend says, "Come, let us find some cheese and a good book to read."

They sit on a chair near the wall of the glacier and they eat and they laugh.

A small bird comes to the cold water on the glacier and it is funny to see.

The bird is not happy, and it does not want to come close to the ice.

Then the sun starts to go down, and the cold is so loud and slow in the park.

The girl and her friend sleep in the chair and they do not forget this cold day in the glacier.

I know a good sculptor. His name is Old Man Stone.

He is not old, but his hands are very old and callused from all his work.

Old Man Stone makes big, funny statues out of cold stone in his garden.

One day, a friend asks him, "Do you love your work?"

Old Man Stone looks up at his big cat statue and he smiles a very big smile.

He is proud of his cat. The cat stands tall and strong on the green wall.

A small bird comes to the garden and the bird sees the cat statue too.

The bird sits on the old tree and it is very quiet there.

Old Man Stone and his cat statue look out at the sun and the warm rain.

He is proud of his work, and the cat is happy, and the garden is good.

Some people come to his garden to see the big cat, and they all stop and smile.

One dog runs up to Old Man Stone and he gives the dog some food from a box.

The dog is hungry, but Old Man Stone is kind, and he help the dog find more food too.

He is proud of his kind hand, and the dog is happy and warm in his room at home.

Old Man Stone sits in his chair, he reads a book, and he thinks of a new story for his next statue.

The sun is warm and the day is good.

There is a big orchard out in the park, with all the trees close together.

The orchard has so many trees, and each tree is green and big.

A girl walks into the orchard and looks at all the trees.

She sees red and blue and green apples on the trees.

The apples are very sweet, and the girl is happy.

She picks one apple and eats it, and she feels warm and happy.

A dog runs up to her and the girl laughs, for the dog is hungry too.

She gives the dog some food, and he is very kind and quiet.

Then she finds a small bird in the orchard, and the bird is not sad or loud.

The girl sits down on a old chair by the wall and reads a book.

The warm sun and the quiet orchard make her feel good.

She thinks that this is a very good day, and she wants to come here again.

The orchard is like a new home for her, warm and kind.

She closes her book and smiles, for the orchard is here, and so is she.

The night is cold and the rain comes down on the old house. A small lantern stands on the table, and it gives a warm, yellow light. The lantern is red and blue, and it is very funny to look at. A woman sit in a chair by the lantern and read a good story. She is happy here, close to the warm light. Her dog and her cat come in too, and they sit by the lantern. The dog is hungry, so the woman open a box of food and give some to him. Then she find a key on the floor, and she ask the cat to help her find more. The cat is slow, but he is kind, and he carry the key to the old door. The lantern light is good, and it help them see the room. The woman laugh and say, "You two love this old house!" When the sun come out in the morning, the lantern is cold and the story is not in the book. But the woman smile, and she know that the night with the warm light was very good.

The boy and his dog go out to find a new park to play in.

But the dog does not know the way, and the boy feels sad.

The boy has a small compass. It is old and blue, and it always points the way.

He look at the compass, and it shows him a green garden.

He and the dog walk into the garden, and the dog is very happy there.

Then the dog run to a big tree, and the boy ask him to stop.

The boy see a funny bird in a red box close to the tree.

He need to get the key to open the box, but he do not have it.

He look at his compass again. The compass is in his hand, and it is warm.

He find a old book on the floor of the garden, and in it there is the key!

He open the box, and the bird jump out and fly into the tree.

The dog laugh, and the boy smile too, for the compass help him find all this good story.

The boy walks to the big canyon in the morning. The canyon is very wide and very deep. He stands at the edge and looks down. He feels awed, like he is too small to see all of it. The canyon is so big that it makes him feel sad and happy at one day. He asks his friend to come and look too. His friend is awed here, and she says yes to his hand. They walk all the way to the old house at the back of the canyon. The old house has a big window and a door, and they sit in the room to read. The boy finds a book on the table and starts to read a story. In the story, a cat and a dog run into the canyon to find water. The dog is quick, but the cat is slow, and the cat gets the fish. They laugh and laugh. Then the rain comes down, and they run out to the canyon wall to get more food. The cold water on the wall is very good. They sit close and eat some cheese and a small red box of food. The boy is so awed by the canyon that he wants to call it "Home of the Big Wall." He and his friend smile, and they sleep in the quiet, warm room.

The old cottage on the green street is small, but it is very warm and happy.

A woman live in the cottage. Her name is Ida, and she is kind and funny.

Ida have a big cat, a dog, and one old bird, and they all love the cottage.

The cottage is not new. It is old, but it is good and homey.

In the morning, Ida and her dog go out to the garden to find food for the bird.

The bird like the garden, and the dog like the garden too. He run and jump there quick.

Ida sit in her chair inside the cottage and read a book by the window.

She hear the rain on the roof, and she smile. The cottage is close and warm.

The cat hide in a box on the floor, and the dog knock on the door of the box too.

Ida laugh and say, "No, no, dog, you need to sleep!"

Then she open a box and find a key inside. A very old key. And she feel happy.

She go out to the park to find a new house for the bird, but the bird not come.

So Ida call the bird home, and the bird come back to the old cottage.

Now the cat, the dog, the bird, and Ida all live in the little cottage, and they feel very good there.

The waterfall is big and loud. Water falls down from a high hill into a green garden. A small cat sits by the waterfall and looks at the water. The cat is very happy to see the water. A dog runs up to the cat and asks for food. The cat gives him some old cheese, but the dog does not stop. He wants more, and more, and more! The cat starts to feel sad, but then she finds a funny bird in a tree. The bird has a red hat and a blue box. The cat and the dog look at the bird and laugh. The bird is kind and good, so the cat and the dog smile again. They all sit by the waterfall and read a story book. The water is cold and the sun is warm. It is a very good day at the waterfall. The cat, the dog, and the bird live close to the waterfall, and they come here to play every morning.

The girl sit at the window and look out at the garden.

She have a old telescope. A big, red telescope with a funny hat on it.

She want to see more of the world, so she open the telescope and look through it.

She see the tree, the bird, the cat, all close to her house.

Then she find a small mouse run out of a box in the garden.

She laugh and say, "I like this telescope very much!"

Her friend come to the door and ask, "Do you have a new telescope?"

No, she say, but she tell him a story of the old one and the funny hat.

They go out into the park and look at the sun and the moon with the telescope again.

The telescope help them see every small star in the night sky.

The dog and the cat and the bird all stand there and look too, and they feel happy.

The girl and her friend sit on the old chair, eat some food, and read a good book.

They do not need more work, they do not need more food, they do not need more. They only need to look and smile.

And every morning, they come to the window and start to wonder, again and again, what is out there in the world.

The cat sits by the window and looks out at the garden. She is vigilant. She does not stop looking, not for one day, not for one night. The dog is vigilant too, and he stands close to the door. They are vigilant because there is a small mouse in the old house. The mouse is hungry and he looks for food in every room. The cat sees the mouse by the table and she makes a loud sound. The mouse runs, but the dog is quick and he catches him in the green garden. The cat and the dog are happy, but the mouse is sad. The mouse wants to find more food, so he is vigilant now too, and he looks in every box and by every chair. The cat and the dog laugh, because the mouse is so small and so funny. They give him some old cheese and a small fish, and he stops and sits down. The two friends tell him the story of their day, and the mouse starts to feel warm and good. He is not sad now. He is vigilant, yes, but he is happy too.

The cat is very hungry. So hungry that she is ravenous, like she needs to eat all day and all night.

She is ravenous after her big fish, and she looks at the door.

A small bird in the tree is ravenous too, and it is not happy.

The bird is ravenous for a blue key, a red hat, and a good book.

The cat and the bird sit close to the window and look out at the garden.

Then the dog comes home and he is ravenous. He runs to the table and he is very loud.

The dog is so ravenous that he is quick and funny, and he makes the cat laugh.

The cat and the dog find some food and they eat fast, but the bird is sad.

The bird says, "I do not have food here. I need help!"

The cat and the dog look at the bird, and then they give her some cheese.

The bird is very kind and she tell them a new story, and they all smile.

Now the cat, the dog, and the bird sit in the warm room and they sleep, ravenous no more.

The night is very quiet, and the old house is cold and dark.

But then I see a small light in the room. A luminous blue bird, like the sun in a box.

The bird is luminous and warm, and it makes the dark room feel good.

I walk close to it and it does not run or hide.

A luminous fish jumps out of the water in the garden, and I have to laugh.

The fish is not bad. It is funny and kind, like a small, hungry friend.

I call it "Lumi" and it comes to me by the old tree.

Lumi and the bird sit on the wall and look at the rain.

I find a book on the table and I read a story to them, and they do not ask for more food or cheese.

The bird is luminous again in the morning, big and beautiful, and it stands at the window.

I open the door and Lumi the fish swim into the park with me.

We play in the green water and I feel happy and slow, like a good day at home.

The luminous bird in the old house? It is here now too, and it smiles at us from the wall.

The night is quiet. Too quiet. The garden is dark and cold. A small bird lives in the old tree. This bird is nocturnal. It comes out at night when all the day birds sleep. The nocturnal bird is green and blue and very funny to look at. It does not sit in the tree all day. It hunts for food at night and sleeps in the day. A cat in the house hear the bird and wake up. The cat is hungry, but the bird is quick and quiet. The cat run to the window, then stop. The nocturnal bird is up in the tree, and the cat is down on the floor. The cat think and then go to sleep again. The bird is happy. It is good to be quiet and warm in the night. The cat and the bird do not say a word. In the morning, the bird fly out of the tree and into the garden. The cat is sad, but the bird is happy. The bird find some food and eat. Then the bird sit in the tree and tell a new story to the quiet night.

The morning sun is warm and the garden is quiet. The lake is tranquil, so still that the birds do not jump or run. Two small birds sit on the old tree by the water and they do not make a loud sound. The lake is tranquil like a big green glass. A cat walks out of the house and she stops at the door of the garden. She looks at the tranquil lake and she feels very happy. The cat is hungry, so she goes to find food. She sees a red box on the table and she is not quick. A dog runs up to the box and the cat calls to him. The dog is kind and he does not catch the cat. They sit down in the warm sun and eat some cheese and fish. The cat and the dog feel good now. A girl in a blue hat comes to the window and she smiles at them. She asks, "Do you like this tranquil morning?" The cat and the dog look at her with big, happy eyes. Yes, she says, and they smile too. They all sit and read a good story, and the day is very good.

The sun is warm and the rain is not here. A small dog and a boy walk out of the house and into the garden.

The dog does not run fast. He walks slow, and the boy walks slow too, and they do not stop. They meander from the garden to the big green field.

To meander is to walk slow and not go right to one new place, but to look at all the old trees and the blue sky and the green water.

The boy is happy to meander, and the dog is happy to meander too, for he is a slow, kind dog.

They see a bird in a big tree, and they stop and look. Then they start to meander again, this time to the park.

At the park, a cat sits on a chair and a mouse hides in a box. The dog and the boy laugh, and they feel good.

The boy asks the dog to help him find a key, and the dog does not know the name of the key, but he is funny and kind.

They walk slow home, and the boy reads a story to the dog. The dog sits on the floor and looks at him with his big green eye.

The boy says, "I love you, old slow dog," and the dog gives them a warm smile with his happy eye.

They eat some food at the table, and the dog and the boy sleep in the warm room. It is a very good day.

The cat is very hungry, and she needs to find some food.

She is furtive. She moves so quiet and slow that no one can hear her.

The cat is furtive when she walks, like a small mouse that wants to hide.

She goes around the room, furtive and quick, and she does not make a sound.

She sees a big fish on the table, but she needs to be quiet to get it.

The cat is furtive at the door, close to the wall, so the dog do not see her.

She is furtive at the window too, and she is not here now, not one word.

The dog look out the window and he start to think. He ask his friend, "Do you know, I see a cat!"

The cat is furtive and she is not happy when the dog look at her.

She jump up and down, and then she run to the garden, furtive and funny.

There she find a small bird, and she give it some food. Good and kind.

The bird is happy, and the cat is happy too, for a day in the sun.

They sit close, and the cat tell a new story to her new friend.

The sky is very dark and the clouds are big and low. It is an ominous day, like the sky is not happy with the earth. The old cat in the house does not like ominous days, so she sits by the window and looks out. A big bird stands on the tree in the garden, and the tree is very old and very quiet. The bird is not funny or loud on an ominous day. It is too sad and slow. The dog runs to the door and then runs back, and the cat asks him to stop. The dog can hear a loud wind and he feels that the day is not good. The cat and the dog find a small room near the wall to hide in. They get some food from the table. There is old cheese and a fish, and the cat is very hungry. The dog wants to eat too, but the cat says no. She is not kind, but she is hungry too. The two friends sit close and they start to feel warm. The ominous sky turns from dark blue to a cold, quiet blue. The rain comes down, but it is not bad rain. It is the kind that makes the garden green and good. The dog and the cat open a box and find a new story book. They read and laugh, and they sleep here, happy and warm, when the sun comes back to the morning.

The sun is up and the day is warm. A small cat sits at the table and looks at the food. The cat is voracious. She eats all the fish and all the cheese very quick. She is so voracious that she does not stop for water. A dog comes into the room and sees the cat. The dog is hungry too, but he is not voracious like the cat. The dog is slow to eat and slow to finish. The cat looks up at the dog with big, funny eyes. The dog wants to play, but the cat wants to eat. They stand close to one another, two hungry friends. The cat is voracious again at night, and she eats more food than a small bird can find in the garden. The dog and the cat sit on the floor and read a story book. They feel happy now, not sad or bad, but warm and kind. A new day is here, and the voracious cat starts to look for more food again. The dog gives her some food, and they smile and sleep.

I find a small fossil in the garden. It is an old bone from a bird that lived long ago. I look at it and I feel very happy. The fossil is dry and cold, and it is not bad at all. I tell my friend, but she do not know what it is. We sit down and we read a book about fossils. A fossil is like a story that the ground keep. I dig more and more in the garden, and I find two more fossils. One is a small fish, and one is a big cat. I put all the fossils in a box and I keep them at home. My dog and my cat want to eat the box, but I say no! I love this old story that the ground give me. I call it "the blue bird fossil" and I give it a funny name. I go out every morning to look at it, and the sun and the rain help it be clean and good. It is a good and kind fossil, and I care for it like that.

The garden is green and warm on a sunny morning.

In the garden, there is a small hedgehog. He is round and brown and very funny to look at.

The hedgehog has big eyes and a funny hat.

He walks slow and quiet, and he does not make a loud sound.

A girl in the park sees the hedgehog and wants to play with him.

She goes close to him and he jumps up and runs to the wall.

The girl laughs and says, "I like you, little hedgehog!"

She gives him some food. Red and blue cheese. And he is very happy.

The hedgehog eats the cheese and then asks for more.

He is so hungry that he stops to find water at the old tree.

A cat in the house hear him and comes out to catch him, but the hedgehog is too quick.

The cat runs and the hedgehog runs too, and they play all day.

At night, the hedgehog goes back to his small box in the garden and sleeps warm.

The girl is sad when he sleep, but then she smile and think, "I love this funny little hedgehog."

The girl find an old box in her garden. She open it and see a small key. She look at the key all morning, but she do not know its name. Then she see the key start to shimmer. It is like light and gold and blue all together. She laugh and hold it close. She walk to her house and put the key on the table. She ask her dog to come, and he come quick. The dog sit down, and the key start to shimmer more and more. The dog look up at her with big, happy eyes. She tell him his name is now Key. She laugh again, and the key shimmer very loud. She run to the window and see the sun go down. The key shimmer in the warm, quiet night. She sit in her chair and read a story to him. The key shimmer on the table, and they feel very happy there. She give him some food, and he is not sad or hungry or bad. A small mouse run out of the old box and hide in the room. She smile and say, "You too, old friend, come and play!" The key shimmer one more time, and then they all sleep.

The road to the park is serpentine. It bends and twists and does not go straight at all.

A car drives on the serpentine road, and the driver has to turn left, then right, then left again.

A small dog runs on the serpentine path too, and he is very quick.

The dog is so hungry that he runs to find food, but the food is not there.

He looks in the garden, he looks by the tree, and he looks at the old house.

A cat sits on a chair and reads a book. She does not want to help the dog.

The dog is sad, but then he finds a funny blue box close to the wall.

Inside the box there is a big, warm, good food. Some cheese and some fish!

The dog is very happy, and he eats all the food slow and quiet.

The cat smiles and says, "I love this serpentine road. It is a good path to find a friend."

The dog and the cat sit by the window and they look out at the sun in the morning.

They do not need to run or jump now. They can sleep and feel warm.

A bird comes to the old tree, and they all hear the loud, kind rain start to fall.

The dog and the cat close their eyes, and they sleep like two small, happy mice.

The old cat sat on the floor of the house, and she did not move. She looked very forlorn, like a small bird with no home and no friend. The girl in the red hat walked into the room and saw the forlorn cat look out the window at the cold rain. "Do you want some food?" she said, and she gave the cat a small box of fish. But the cat did not eat. She was too sad and forlorn to eat. The girl sat down on the chair and read a book to the cat, and they sat there all morning. Then the girl found a funny old dog in the garden, and the dog ran into the house and jumped up at the cat. The cat laughed. Or she looked funny, like a cat that does not know dogs. Now the cat and the dog play in the park again and again. The girl is happy, and the cat is not forlorn any more. She has a new friend, and that is very good.

The morning sun is good and warm, but then the cold rain start to come down. The rain is not big or loud, but it is not small or quiet too. Then the snow start to fall, and the snow is very big and very cold. The snow and the rain make a blizzard. A blizzard is a very bad day when all the cold and the snow come in and you can not go out. The blizzard is so big that the whole town is quiet. A small dog and his friend the cat do not want to go out into the blizzard. They hide in the old house and eat some food. The dog is hungry, and he ask his friend to give him more food. The cat say no, but then she see a funny bird close to the window. The bird is cold and sad, and the cat want to help her. They open the door of the house and the bird come in fast. Now the two friends feel happy, and they sit by the warm room. The blizzard is loud and big outside, but inside it is quiet and good. The dog and the cat read a new story book and sleep. The bird live in a box on the table, and she smile with her eye. They all know that the blizzard will stop soon, and the sun will come again.

The old house on Green Street has a big cellar. A small room down in the cold, dark floor. No one in the house likes to go into the cellar. The door to the cellar is old and very quiet. One day, a girl finds a key on the floor of the cellar. She is not afraid now. She has a good friend, and her name is Cat. Cat is a funny little dog, and he is not hungry. The girl and Cat open the door of the cellar and look in. There is a big table in the cellar, and on the table there is an old book, some food, and a red hat. The book has a funny story in it. The girl and Cat sit down in the cold, dark room and read. They do not feel sad or bad here. The cellar is not so bad when you have a friend and a good book. The next morning, the girl and Cat find more old things in the cellar. A blue fish, a green cat, and a small box of cheese. They all smile and say, "We love this old house and this funny cellar!"

The cat and the dog play in the garden all morning.

The dog is big and quick, but the cat is small and funny.

One day, the cat sits on a chair and asks, "Can you solve this?"

She gives the dog a riddle: "I have a key but no hand, I have a box but no lid. What am I?"

The dog think and think, but he cannot find the answer.

The cat smiles and says, "I love it when you do not know the answer to a riddle!"

The dog ask again: "Do you have a new riddle for me?"

The cat is very kind, and she tell him, "I have a red book on the table. The last word in that book is the answer to this riddle."

The dog run to the table and see the book, but the riddle is not there.

Then the cat laugh, loud and warm, and the dog feel happy and hungry.

They go into the house, and the cat say, "I need some food. Do you want to help me get it?"

The dog and the cat work together to find the food, and they eat cheese and fish and feel very good.

At night, the cat hide a small bird in a box in the garden, and the dog has to find it.

The dog is slow, but the cat is not sad. She sit by the window and look at him.

They play again on a warm morning when the sun come out and the rain stop.

The old boat in the park is big and heavy, and a small cat sits on it. The cat is an anchor. She does not run or jump, she just sits and holds the boat in one place. A dog runs up to the boat and asks, "Do you want to play?" But the cat is an anchor, and she does not move. A bird comes and sits on the boat too, and then a fish comes out of the water and jumps. The cat is an anchor, and she is very calm. The dog is sad, but the cat is happy and quiet. The dog then finds a old book on the floor and reads a story to the cat. The cat and the dog read and eat some food all morning. When the rain starts to come down, the cat is an anchor. She keeps the boat safe and steady in the cold water. The dog is happy now, and he says, "You are a good friend, I like you." The cat smiles, for she knows that a good anchor does not need to say a word. They sit and hear the rain, and they feel warm and good.

The night was cold and the rain came down. A big fire started in the old house. A blaze of red and blue light up in the room. The blaze was so big that everyone could see it from the street. A dog run out of the house and into the garden. He looked up at the blaze and he did not go in. A cat sat on the wall and she too looked at the blaze. The fire was very bad, but the dog and the cat did not want to help. A small bird in the tree had no food and no warm home. The bird was hungry and sad. Then a kind friend found a box of old food by the door. She gave the food to the bird, and the bird gave her a funny look. The friend smiled and said, "I love you, small bird." The bird and the friend sit close to the warm wall, and they eat and they read a good story. The blaze in the old house is now quiet, and the morning sun comes up. The dog and the cat come in, and they all sleep, happy and warm, in one room.

The girl find a small pebble by the old tree in the garden.

The pebble is red and blue and green, and it look very funny.

She throw the pebble into the water, and it go slow and cold.

A bird see the pebble and come to the water too.

The bird want to eat the pebble, but the girl say no.

She keep the pebble in her hand, and she feel happy.

Her dog run to her and ask for the pebble with his big eye.

She laugh and give him some food, but he not happy.

Then she find a new small pebble by the door, and it is very smooth and kind.

She and the dog play with the pebble all day in the park.

At night, she put the pebble in her box by the window, for she love it like a friend.

The next morning, the pebble not there, and she feel sad.

She look in the room, she look in the garden, but she need to find it.

She ask her old cat, and the cat tell her a story with one word: home.

The cat help her find the pebble, and they all go home happy.

The big river runs through the park, and the water is very cold and loud.

By the river, there is a wide, deep gorge. So big and so dark that the sun does not come in.

Two friends stand at the edge of the gorge and look down into it.

The gorge is so wide that they need to walk all the way to the wall to see the other side.

One friend says, "This gorge is very, very big!"

They find a old chair by the door of a small house close to the gorge, and they sit down.

The boy with the red hat asks his friend, "Do we need to go into the gorge? Yes, we do!"

They open a box of food and eat some cheese and fish and laugh.

A dog runs up and the cat jumps out of the room, and they all laugh again.

The dog is hungry, and the cat is sad, but the boy gives them some food too.

They read a book by the window and hear the rain come down on the gorge.

The rain is loud in the big, deep gorge, and it makes the water jump and run fast.

They close the book and look out at the quiet morning sun.

The gorge is not a bad place to live. It is a funny, good, kind place to work and play.

The old barn stood at the edge of the farm, big and quiet.

It was a rustic barn, made of old wood and green rain.

A small cat and a dog live in the barn, and they do not like the cold.

The cat is hungry, so she go to the door and look for food.

The dog find a box of old cheese on the floor of the barn.

The cat say, "This is a good day!" and she eat all the cheese quick.

Then the dog run to the wall and call out a funny name for the cat.

The cat laugh, and they play in the big room of the barn.

One morning, a bird come in through the open window and sit on the table.

The dog and the cat see the bird and they want to catch it, but the bird is too slow.

The cat jump up to the wall, the dog run to the door, but the bird is not here now.

They sit down and look at one another, and they feel sad.

But then the dog find a new key in the old box, and the barn start to feel warm again.

The cat and the dog smile, and they know the barn is a good home for them.

A small child on the street ask, "Do you have a friend here?" and the dog smile and say, "Yes, his name is Cat, and we love this old barn."

The rain stops and the sun comes out. I walk to the meadow at the end of the street. The meadow is big and green and full of flowers. A small bird sits in the tree close to the meadow, and it looks very happy. I find a old chair by the water and sit down. Then I see a light in the distance. It is a beacon, a bright blue and red light on top of a tall old house far away. The beacon is like a kind friend that calls out to me on a cold day. I think: a beacon of hope, here in this quiet meadow. I open a book and read a story to the bird in the tree. The dog runs up and the cat jumps down, and we all laugh. I feel warm and good now, and the meadow is a good place to rest. The beacon in the old house stands quiet and kind, and I smile at it. I do not need to hide or run. I have the meadow, I have the book, and I have this happy day. I ask the dog to help me find the way home, and we walk slow and close, one hand in the meadow and one hand in the rain. But no, we do not go home. We stop at the beacon and sit. It is a very good day, and I smile again.

The old house on the street has a big cellar. A dark, cold room down below the floor.

No one knows how old the cellar is, but they say the house is very old.

One day, a small boy find a lantern in the garden. It is blue and red and it makes a warm light.

He is hungry, so he go into the cellar to find food. He open a big box and see cheese and fish and more!

The lantern is very good, and the cold room start to feel warm again.

But then he hear a loud, funny sound from the cellar. He stop and look.

He see a cat, a dog, and a mouse all hide in a small room close to the wall.

The boy laugh, but then he think: this is a scary story for a new friend.

He tell his friend a story of the old house, the big cellar, and the funny cat.

His friend ask him to come and help her find a key in the dark cellar one morning.

They work slow and quiet, and the lantern light find the key on the old table.

The two friends laugh and feel happy, and the old house do not feel bad now.

They run out into the park, and the sun is warm and good for the whole day.

The sea is big and cold. I want to go out on a voyage. A long journey on a boat. I ask my friend for a boat, and she gives me an old one. We start our voyage into the blue water. The sea is not loud or bad, but it is far. We sail for a long day. At night, we find a harbor. A small, safe place by the water where boats can stop. The harbor has a big green house and a red door. We walk into the harbor and sit in a warm room. There is food and water and a chair by the window. I feel very happy and hungry. We eat some fish and cheese, and a funny cat jumps up on the table. I laugh and say, "This is a good voyage!" The next morning, we leave the harbor and go out again. I know I will come back to this harbor again and again. A good harbor is like a kind friend.

The sun is warm and the garden is quiet. A small boy walks to the orchard, a big garden of old trees. He loves the orchard, and he goes there every morning. The orchard has green trees, and the trees have red apples. He is ravenous, so hungry that he wants to eat all the food he can find. He is so ravenous that he eats one apple, then two, then more. His friend the dog is ravenous too, and the dog runs to him. The dog is ravenous like a bird in the park, quick and loud. The boy and the dog sit at the table and eat, and they feel very happy. But then the boy finds a fossil. An old, old bone, hidden under the green wall. He is not ravenous now. He is sad and quiet. He looks at the fossil and wants to know its story. He asks his friend, "Do you know this old bone?" His friend is kind and she says, "I think this fossil is here from the old house on this street." The boy and the dog look at the fossil again, and the dog is funny. The boy smiles, and they sit close to the warm sun in the orchard. He does not feel bad. He feels that the fossil is a good new story for them to read.
